app.title: TomaNote
app.slogan: TomaNote: free, private & offline online notepad
app.doodle-subtitle: Open an existing note or create a new one with the
about.main-first-description: TomaNote is a simple, fast, and free notepad designed for distraction-free writing.
about.main-second-description: It runs directly in your browser with no accounts or sign-up required. All your notes are saved locally on your device, meaning you stay in full control of your data.
about.intro-third-description: It's not a complex productivity platform or a project manager. It's a clean space to think, write, and organize ideas.
about.second-first-description: Notes are stored in your browser's local storage. If you clear your browser data or hard-reload the page, your notes will be lost. We recommend exporting or backing up your notes regularly.
about.title-second-list-item4: Dark mode by default with multiple customization themes
about.third-first-description: TomaNote exists because writing should be simple, free, and accessible — no external services or subscriptions needed.
seo.about.title: About - TomaNote
seo.about.description: Learn about TomaNote - a simple, fast, and free notepad designed for distraction-free writing. No accounts required, notes stored locally.

terms.main-subtitle: Terms and Warnings for Notepad Use
terms.intro: Please read these terms carefully before using TomaNote.
terms.second-description1: This notepad uses your browser's LocalStorage to save data. Although the storage limit is 5 MB, we recommend avoiding large volumes of text. Browser storage is limited and may be affected by saving large amounts of data. If you reach the limit, you may lose data — please back up your notes regularly.
terms.tip-export: Tip: Export your notes regularly to avoid data loss
terms.second-description2: Data saved in this notepad is not encrypted and is stored in your browser's LocalStorage, which does not guarantee security. We recommend not storing sensitive or private information here, as it may be vulnerable to unauthorized access if others have access to your device or browser.
terms.security-note: Security note: Never store passwords, credit card details, or other sensitive information
terms.second-description3: Make sure you are using a trusted and secure URL when accessing the notepad. Avoid accessing it from suspicious links or unsecured public networks, as you may be exposed to phishing attacks or data theft. The development team is not responsible for data loss due to improper use or insecure access.
terms.url-check: ✓ Always check the URL is correct
terms.license-description: TomaNote is open source software licensed under
terms.license-open-source: Open source • Free to use • Community driven
terms.license-agreement: By using TomaNote, you acknowledge that you have read, understood, and agree to be bound by these terms.
seo.terms.title: Terms of Use - TomaNote
seo.terms.description: TomaNote terms of use - Storage limits, data security, and URL safety guidelines.
privacy.intro: At TomaNote, we value your privacy. This policy explains how your data is handled.
privacy.storage-description: All your notes are stored locally in your browser's LocalStorage. We do not have any servers that store your data. This means:
privacy.storage-item1: Your notes never leave your device
privacy.storage-item3: We do not collect any personal information
privacy.storage-item4: No cookies are used for tracking
privacy.no-account-description: TomaNote works completely anonymously. No email, no registration, no account needed. You are in full control of your data.
privacy.security-description: Data saved in this notepad is stored in your browser's LocalStorage, which does not guarantee encryption.
privacy.security-recommendation: We recommend not storing sensitive or private information.
privacy.changes-description: We may update this policy from time to time. Any changes will be posted on this page.
seo.privacy.title: Privacy Policy - TomaNote
seo.privacy.description: TomaNote privacy policy - Your notes are stored locally in your browser. No data is collected or transmitted.

_comment_roadmap: Auto-generated from roadmap-data.json - Do not edit manually
roadmap.prev.0.description: The native browser confirm() is being phased out, so a custom confirmation modal was implemented, aligned with the app's design.
roadmap.prev.1.description: The floating menu appeared in different positions between the web version and the installed PWA, so a dynamic script was implemented to calculate the correct position in both contexts.
roadmap.prev.2.description: Tests cover new and modified features. The tab closing logic was moved to a modular standalone script, and tests were added for the floating menu and tab close confirmation.
roadmap.prev.3.description: The 'alert' and 'trash' icons were added to provide a clearer representation of the tab closing flow for users.
roadmap.prev.4.description: The text editor now includes global styles for consistent rendering, especially optimized for content pasted from external web pages.
roadmap.current.0.description: All components restructured into src/features/ as self-contained modules: FloatingMenu, ContextualMenu, ModalInfo, CreateTab, CloseTabConfirmation, and Roadmap
roadmap.current.1.description: Custom <dialog> element for tab deletion, replacing the native browser confirm()
roadmap.current.2.description: ARIA attributes on modals, improved keyboard navigation, screen reader support
roadmap.current.3.description: Google Search Console verification meta tag, BreadcrumbList and HowTo schemas, 3 new SEO views, default hreflang set to 'en', AI agent indexing (Google, GPT, Anthropic)
roadmap.current.4.description: Floating menu position auto-adjusts based on install mode (PWA vs browser), tablet and mobile display corrections, pasted text preserves its original structure
roadmap.current.5.description: All comments and test descriptions translated to English, contextual menu and confirmation modal translation
roadmap.current.6.description: Removed outline on [contenteditable]:focus, added global dialog::backdrop, --nav-bottom adjusted to 1rem, removed redundant font styles
roadmap.current.7.description: 6 icons added: close button, rocket, file-text, chevron-right, alert, and trash
roadmap.current.8.description: New Roadmap tab replacing the News tab, with i18n integration and automated sync from roadmap-data.json
roadmap.current.9.description: Scripts for automated roadmap translation sync and CHANGELOG.md generation
roadmap.current.10.description: Removed orphaned scripts (roadmap-gen.js), unused roadmap.old-* translation keys, duplicate files deleted
roadmap.next.0.description: Keyboard shortcuts for the app's most common actions: creating a note, navigating between notes, opening the menu and settings, among others.
roadmap.next.1.description: Write in Markdown and see the result rendered in real time directly in your note.
roadmap.next.2.description: Search among your notes, either by title or directly by note content.
roadmap.prev.5.description: All comments and test descriptions translated to English, contextual menu and confirmation modal translation
roadmap.prev.6.description: Removed outline on [contenteditable]:focus, added global dialog::backdrop, --nav-bottom adjusted to 1rem, removed redundant font styles
roadmap.prev.7.description: 6 icons added: close button, rocket, file-text, chevron-right, alert, and trash
roadmap.prev.8.description: New Roadmap tab replacing the News tab, with i18n integration and automated sync from roadmap-data.json
roadmap.prev.9.description: Scripts for automated roadmap translation sync and CHANGELOG.md generation
roadmap.prev.10.description: Removed orphaned scripts (roadmap-gen.js), unused roadmap.old-* translation keys, duplicate files deleted
roadmap.next.3.description: Pre-loading system when connection fails, showing friendly alerts and offering access to previous versions without data loss.
roadmap.next.4.description: Service worker for automatic connection recovery, intelligent caching and offline mode.
roadmap.next.5.description: New keyboard shortcuts using left_Alt for quick actions, integrated with the command palette.
